mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 5% over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE's predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample tests (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in consumer spending (CPIAUCSL) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weak aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenue growth slows.
failure_condition: Interaction term between CPIAUCSL and HOUST shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.1), or combined declines fail to predict negative returns in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side disruptions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate margins face upward pressure.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in > 50% of INDPRO decline events, or the relationship breaks down during 2008-2009 crisis periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with stagnant monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) creates a 'stagflation' risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns as real rates rise.
failure_condition: Interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows no significant impact on SPX returns (p > 0.1), or 60d returns remain positive despite stagflation conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings improve.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.1), or combined strength fails to predict positive returns in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) during an expansionary phase signals weakening labor market dynamics, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns as recession risks rise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50k in 3 months during expansion
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 50% of PAYEMS decline events during expansions, or the relationship breaks down in 2000-2001 or 2008-2009 periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong supply-side capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate margins expand.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of INDPRO growth periods, or the relationship breaks down during 2008-2009 crisis periods.
